_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised, autopilot local cron)
_meta.note: VA local batch — finish the Portsmouth City Council (Glover/Bryant/Hugel already done in 1530). This batch: Vernon Tillage Jr (at-large, sworn Jan 2023, former Portsmouth Dem Committee chair); William S. Dodson Jr (at-large, sworn Jan 1 2025 via 33-vote recount); William E. 'Bill' Moody Jr (Vice Mayor + Council, 1998-present, 7th term, seeking 8th); Yolanda E. Thomas (at-large, sworn Jan 1 2025). KEY EVIDENCE: (1) Tillage = D-machine identity (former Portsmouth Democratic Committee chair; staffer for Sen. Louise Lucas + Sen. Mark Warner; Hampton Roads Young Democrats VP; Run For Something–endorsed 2020 school-board race) + 'root-causes / task-force' public-health framing on gun violence — but no Pride proclamation vote, no abortion vote, no LGBTQ statement, no school-choice/curriculum stance, no quoted 'public-health crisis' framing in his own words. Sworn Jan 2023 so NOT on council for the 1/14/2020 4-3 2A vote. Per the SKILL's sparse-data discipline + Doyle/Bryant precedent (single-vector progressive rhetoric without independent culture-war votes) — mark roster_verified, leave all 30 cells null; capture progressive-archetype context in notes. Fix party Unknown→I. (2) Dodson = officially nonpartisan endodontist/distillery owner, sworn Jan 1 2025 by 33-vote recount over incumbent De'Andre Barnes; four-pillar campaign frame (crime/infrastructure/economic dev/community) with effectively ZERO culture-war footprint; sole NO on Mar 2025 EMS workers'-comp presumption ordinance (ambiguous motive — not scoreable); NOT on council for the 2020 2A vote (sworn 5 years later); first-term Catholic-school-childhood Independent with no R/D donation history. roster_verified, all 30 cells null. Fix party Unknown→I. (3) Moody = THE counter-archetype catch of this batch. 7-term R-leaning Independent (in his 7th term, 2022-2026; Vice Mayor 2004-2006, 2008-2010, 2025-present); per WAVY May 27 2026 he REVERSED his earlier retirement announcement and IS seeking an 8th term (CORRECTION to the 1530 dossier's stale assumption that he 'is not seeking re-election'). HARD TRUE self_defense q0+q1 on the Jan 14 2020 4-3 'Second Amendment Constitutional City' vote AND publicly defending Councilman Nathan Clark's right to bring an AR-15 to council chambers ('I do not believe that Council[man] Clark needs to apologize for exercising his right as a law enforcement officer to carry his weapon to a public meeting' — direct quote). HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2 on his 2022 platform pledge of 'a safe city with lower taxes' + voting for the FY25 budget using Rivers Casino revenue to keep the real-estate tax rate DECREASE $1.30→$1.23 (same precedent as the Glover 1530 TRUE on q2). HARD TRUE public_justice q0 via multi-vector affirmative pro-police advocacy: emailed Chief Angela Greene July 14 2020 specifically demanding the law be enforced and charges filed against Confederate-monument vandals (NAACP attempted to charge him for the email; magistrate declined); 2022 platform on maintaining 260-sworn-officer staffing + restoring Community Impact Officers; defended Clark; called BLM tunnel-blockers 'thugs' in 2016 (later apologized). HARD TRUE refuse_state_overreach q0 on the 2020 2A vote — explicitly framed as local pushback against the Democratic-trifecta VA General Assembly's gun bills ('if they have a problem with the law they should use the system they now control to change it'). HARD FALSE public_justice q3 — voted YES on Portsmouth's gun-buyback program while admitting 'studies show gun buyback programs aren't effective at obtaining guns from those who will use them to harm others' but 'it can't hurt' (acquiescence to public-health framing he himself didn't believe). Counter-archetype context noted but NOT scored: (a) Moody won an open-government FOIA lawsuit against his own council in 2016 (Moody v. Portsmouth, Judge Hammond) after they fined him $1,500 for a Facebook post disclosing closed-session monument discussions — a clean transparency/free-speech win, but no rubric cell maps to it; (b) defends history/Confederate-monument-relocation skeptic ('Removing history is something I associate with bad government, communist government, fascist government'); (c) negative-evidence flags: 2016 BLM 'thugs' (apologized), 2019 'really tired of hearing the term people of color'/'Squad' tweet, pending Dorris v. Glover/Moody wrongful-termination suit alleging tax-assessment interference at Elizabeth Manor Country Club — these don't contradict his archetype but should be noted in audit trail. Fix party Unknown→I; set candidacy_status=general_candidate (Nov 2026 8th-term bid). (4) Yolanda Thomas = first-term (Jan 2025-Dec 2028, NOT on Nov 2026 ballot) at-large Independent on a nonpartisan ballot; D-aligned by civic network (Delta Sigma Theta, M.A.N. Foundation reentry-board, public-school advocacy, UVa ChemE/PMP/TowneBank-VP credentialed soft-progressive technocrat) but NO formal Dem endorsement located; her 'five-point plan' pairs police 'with community precincts and social services outreach in high-crime, high-need areas' — soft public-health-adjacent framing. NOT on council for the 1/14/2020 2A vote (was on Portsmouth School Board then). No Pride, abortion, sanctuary, ESG, ICE, school-choice, or curriculum vote/quote located. Per Doyle/Bryant precedent — mark roster_verified, leave all 30 cells null pending an accumulating record. Fix party Unknown→I. PORTSMOUTH 2A NOTE (reaffirms 1530): the Jan 14 2020 4-3 'Second Amendment Constitutional City' resolution is the ONE big scoreable 2A roll-call. Members seated then: Mayor Rowe (NO), VM Lucas-Burke (NO), Glover (NO), and four YES votes from then-members. Of THIS batch only Moody was seated and voted YES; Tillage, Dodson, Thomas were not yet on council. PORTSMOUTH PRIDE NOTE: no Portsmouth VA city-council Pride proclamation vote located in any year — first Pride Festival 2022 was a private nonprofit (Portsmouth Pride VA) event sponsored by Sen. Louise Lucas, not a council action. The agent surfaced a 'Portsmouth sanctuary for LGBTQ' resolution but verification showed that was Portsmouth, OHIO (Buckeye Flame), not Virginia. PORTSMOUTH SANCTUARY NOTE (reaffirms 1530): Portsmouth on the May 2025 DHS sanctuary-jurisdictions list; city spokesperson 'researching' rather than disavowing; independently-elected Sheriff Michael Moore cancelled a pending ICE Warrant Service Officer / 287(g) agreement. No personal Tillage/Dodson/Moody/Thomas public statement on either ICE or the sanctuary listing located. STRETCH FLAG: rick-west (VA Mayor of Chesapeake) was picked FIRST by priority but remains SLUG-COLLIDED with rick-west / OK State Rep — engine keys by slug, NOT (state,slug). Continues deferred from 1441 batch; needs a slug-rename helper script (e.g. rick-west → rick-west-chesapeake) before scoring. Flagged again here for future fix. After this batch the Portsmouth City Council (Mayor + 6 at-large) is fully roster-finalized: Glover + Bryant + Hugel from 1530, plus Moody + Tillage + Dodson + Thomas here.

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of a denominational affiliation or church membership. Campaign site uses the generic 'shining city on a hill' phrase (Matthew 5 / Reagan trope; not a personal profession of Christ).
profile.education: Churchland High School (Portsmouth); B.S. Political Science, Old Dominion University (2016); ODU Alumni Association inaugural 'Top 40 Under 40' (2021)
profile.background: Portsmouth native. Career: legislative assistant in the VA General Assembly; staff for Sen. Louise Lucas; outreach representative for U.S. Senator Mark Warner; currently Community Affairs / Community Relations Project Coordinator III at Virginia Natural Gas. Civic: former Portsmouth School Board member (2020 race endorsed by Run For Something, the progressive young-Democrat candidate-development org); former chair of the Portsmouth Democratic Committee at the time of his 2022 council run (chair role has since handed to Yolanda Daughtry per VA Democrats local chapter directory); Hampton Roads Young Democrats vice president; board president of Friends of the Portsmouth Juvenile Court; MLK Leadership Committee executive board; former Parks & Recreation Commission, Complete Count Commission vice-chair, Downtown Design Committee, Volunteers for the Homeless. Portsmouth City Council, at-large (officially nonpartisan), term 1/1/2023-12/31/2026 — ON the Nov 3, 2026 ballot (Wikipedia status: 'potential'; campaign site is active soliciting donations and yard signs; references Nov 3 2026 election); candidacy_status set general_candidate.

vernon-tillage.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Portsmouth City Council, at-large (officially nonpartisan, real-world D-machine identity per former Portsmouth Democratic Committee chair + Sen. Lucas / Sen. Warner staff history + Hampton Roads Young Democrats VP + 2020 Run For Something endorsement). Term 1/1/2023-12/31/2026 — ON Nov 3, 2026 ballot. NOT on council for the 1/14/2020 4-3 'Second Amendment Constitutional City' vote (sworn 3 years later) — no Tillage 2A roll-call to score. Per the SKILL's sparse-data discipline + Doyle/Bryant precedent (single-vector progressive rhetoric on safety without a quoted 'public-health crisis' line or an independent endorsement vector does NOT clear the public_justice q0 high bar, and conversely soft 'root causes' framing without an explicit health-framing quote in his OWN voice is not strong enough to clear FALSE on q3), this record is left roster_verified with all 30 LOCAL-rubric cells null. NO QUOTE-BEARING POSITIONS LOCATED on sanctity of life (no abortion-stance statement), biblical_marriage (any cell — no marriage-definition statement, no trans-policy statement, no Pride statement; Portsmouth has no council Pride proclamation vote on record), family_child_sovereignty (his school-board platform centered on accreditation/CTE/teacher pay — public-school-strengthening, but no quoted opposition to school choice / vouchers / charters / homeschool; RFS is a broadly-progressive recruit-and-train org not a single-issue education group so the endorsement does NOT cleanly cite a school-choice cell per skill discipline — left null), christian_liberty (no profession of Christ, no public-square symbol/prayer/Sabbath advocacy, no pastor-conscience defense, no compelled-speech-pronoun stance), economic_stewardship (he voted AGAINST a 5% water/sewer rate increase and against a $1,500 retiree-stipend consensus — single-issue restraint votes — but his broader posture is pro-collective-bargaining (architect of city-employee push), pro-federal-spending (called federal funding freeze 'devastating' and urged localities to 'push back'), and his affordability framing is subsidies/credits rather than budget caps; routine budget votes are not scored and the restraint vote(s) are offset by expansion posture → left null per discipline), election_integrity (any cell — Portsmouth uses VA standard election infrastructure; no Tillage roll-call or quote located), public_justice q0 (single-vector campaign rhetoric on 'modernized public safety' + 'task force' without an FOP endorsement or anti-defund quote — Doyle-precedent NULL), q1/q2/q4 (no cash-bail / restitution / Romans-13 framing), q3 (his framing favors 'root causes of crime and poverty' and a 'task force' — closest match to public-health framing in this batch among the non-Moody members, but the quote in his OWN voice ('citizens of our community are suffering. People are losing their life. We need a task force…') stops short of the explicit 'public health crisis' phrase the rubric scores against; left null per strict cite-only discipline), refuse_state_overreach (any cell — his Jan 2025 'push back' against the federal funding freeze is anti-federal-restraint rather than anti-federal-overreach, opposite of the rubric's subsidiarity sense; not scoreable), border_immigration q1/q2 (Portsmouth on the May 2025 DHS sanctuary list and the city's only response was 'researching' rather than disavowing; Tillage has NO public statement on either ICE cooperation or the sanctuary listing — left null per skill discipline rather than inferring from city posture or Dem-machine identity), self_defense q0/q1 (not seated for the 2020 2A vote; no subsequent firearms ordinance roll-call located during his tenure). Counter-archetype context: D-machine operator with progressive endorsement history (Run For Something 2020, Hampton Roads Young Democrats VP, Sen. Lucas/Warner staff lineage), pro-collective-bargaining, pro-federal-spending posture; soft on public-safety framing without an explicit defund or public-health quote in his own voice; record is so silent on cultural cells that absence-of-evidence reflects absence-of-public-position, not just sparse research. Fix party Unknown→I (Portsmouth races are officially nonpartisan; real-world alignment is Democratic). Recommend revisiting after the Portsmouth FY27 budget vote, the Nov 2026 election, and any 2026 council action on Pride / sanctuary / abortion / firearms.

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of current denominational affiliation. Attended St. Teresa Elementary in Portsmouth (Catholic parochial school) 1963-1970 — childhood schooling, NOT an affirmative current-faith profession.
profile.education: St. Teresa Elementary (Portsmouth, 1963-1970); University of Florida (D.M.D., dentistry); New York University (post-doctoral certificate in Endodontics); additional degrees/certificates in Mathematics, Psychology, and Aerospace Engineering
profile.background: Portsmouth native, married Yvonne Marks Dodson since 1983 (43 years); 2 children, 4 grandchildren. Career: aerospace engineer on F-15/F-16/F-22 programs (1983-1994); programmer at Hughes Aircraft (1982-1983); since 2000 a practicing endodontist in private practice (revenue >$1M/month per campaign bio). Founder/operator of 8 Shires Coloniale Distillery in Williamsburg (2014-present). Portsmouth City Council, at-large (officially nonpartisan), elected Nov 5, 2024 via a 33-vote recount margin (11,756-11,723) over incumbent De'Andre Barnes; sworn 1/1/2025; term 1/1/2025-12/31/2028 — NOT on the Nov 2026 ballot. Ran as Independent on a nonpartisan ballot; no R/D donor history located on VPAP; no Family Foundation / NRA / VCDL / Moms Demand Action / LGBT Victory Fund / Sierra Club / Sheriff endorsement located either direction.

william-dodson.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Portsmouth City Council, at-large (officially nonpartisan, ran as Independent). Term 1/1/2025-12/31/2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. NOT on council for the 1/14/2020 4-3 'Second Amendment Constitutional City' vote (sworn 5 years later). His entire public policy footprint is the four-pillar campaign frame — Crime / Education / Infrastructure / Economic Development — with effectively zero culture-war or values-track content on his campaign site, in candidate forums, in news coverage, or in available council statements. Catholic parochial elementary school in his bio is the closest religious data point and does not establish current faith affiliation. His one identifiable contrarian council vote (sole NO on the Mar 25 2025 6-1 vote granting VA Code §65.2-402(B) workers'-comp presumption to salaried Portsmouth EMS personnel) is ambiguous in motive (cost-benefit vs. principled opposition) and not scoreable. His police-collaboration language ('Crime can't be reduced without a fully staffed police force and more collaboration with the commonwealth's attorney's office') is single-vector campaign rhetoric — no independent FOP endorsement, no anti-defund quote — and does NOT clear the public_justice q0 high bar per Doyle-precedent. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity of life (any cell), biblical_marriage (any cell — no statement on natural marriage, biological-sex/trans policy, Pride proclamations; Portsmouth has no council Pride proclamation vote on record), family_child_sovereignty (any cell — 'Education for Empowerment' priority is unspecified), christian_liberty (any cell — no profession of Christ in office, no defense of public-square Christian symbols/prayer, no pastor/conscience advocacy, no compelled-speech-pronoun stance), economic_stewardship (FY26 budget retained the $1.24/$100 tax rate but routine budget approval is not scored; no standalone tax-cut advocacy or anti-spending vote located — he campaigns on 'economic development' and 'infrastructure' growth, not restraint), election_integrity (any cell — notably, after losing the initial count he was BENEFICIARY of a recount that confirmed his win by 33 votes, so he had reason to comment on election processes, but no anti-fraud/ID/audit statement located), public_justice q0 (Doyle-precedent NULL on single-vector staffing rhetoric), q1/q2/q4 (no cash-bail / restitution / Romans-13 framing), q3 (no statement rejecting the public-health framing of crime; he endorsed the city's existing Crime and Gun Violence Prevention Commission framework approvingly — soft signal against, but no quoted framing in his own voice → left null), refuse_state_overreach (any cell — no statements on COVID, vaccine mandates, ESG, federalism), border_immigration q1/q2 (no Dodson statement on Portsmouth's May 2025 DHS sanctuary listing or Sheriff Moore's WSO/287(g) cancellation), self_defense q0/q1 (not seated for the 2020 2A vote; no subsequent firearms ordinance roll-call; no NRA/VCDL endorsement; no gun-rights statement). Counter-archetype context: NONE — he is a genuinely low-signal, technocratic, locally-focused independent. Endodontist + aerospace engineer + distillery owner with a deep technical background, the cleanest 'truly Independent' record in this batch. Fix party Unknown→I. Recommend revisiting after FY27 budget cycle and any 2026 council action on Pride / sanctuary / abortion / firearms.

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of a specific denominational affiliation or church home. profile.education: Western Branch High School (Portsmouth); U.S. Air Force Loadmaster Flight School; Alpha College of Real Estate (Virginia real-estate licensure); Dale Carnegie Public Speaking
profile.background: Portsmouth native, one of nine children. U.S. Air Force veteran (Loadmaster). 28 years at Doughtie's Foods (Port Norfolk), retired as VP Sales; then 10 years at U.S. Food Service Virginia Beach Division. Licensed Virginia real estate agent. Married to Mary Catterton (49 years); three children. Portsmouth City Council at-large (officially nonpartisan), continuously elected since 1998 — re-elected 2002, 2006, 2010, 2014, 2018, 2022 (current 7th four-year term 1/1/2023-12/31/2026). Vice Mayor 2004-2006, 2008-2010, and 2025-present (appointed by council Jan 2025; replaced VM Lisa Lucas-Burke who ran against Mayor Glover in 2024). Self-describes as the longest consecutively-serving councilmember in Portsmouth history. **Candidacy update (overrides 1530 dossier's stale assumption that he is not seeking re-election):** per WAVY May 27 2026 Moody REVERSED course and confirmed he WILL seek an 8th term in Nov 2026 — at least his third retirement reversal (also retracted in 2022 to seek his 7th term). Quoted: 'we have too much going on, especially with the waterfront development, to leave.' candidacy_status set general_candidate. Cross-cutting record: cultural-conservative populist (pro-2A, pro-police, pro-history, pro-transparency) operating in an officially-nonpartisan office. Counter-flag context: 2016 BLM-tunnel-protester 'thugs' remark (later apologized); 2019 'really tired of hearing the term people of color' / 'Squad' tweet drew NAACP rebuke; named defendant in Patrick Dorris v. Glover/Moody wrongful-termination suit alleging tax-assessment interference at Elizabeth Manor Country Club (unproven, pending). These don't contradict his archetype but are documented in the audit trail.

william-moody.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Portsmouth Vice Mayor + City Council, at-large (officially nonpartisan, R-leaning Independent by issue profile). Continuously elected since 1998, 7 confirmed terms; current term 1/1/2023-12/31/2026; PER WAVY 5/27/2026 he reversed course and IS seeking an 8th term in Nov 2026 (overrides the 1530 dossier's stale assumption). candidacy_status set general_candidate. HARD TRUE self_defense q0 + q1 evidence: as a long-serving councilmember he was a YES vote on the 1/14/2020 4-3 'Second Amendment Constitutional City' resolution declaring Portsmouth a 2A sanctuary, and publicly defended Councilman Nathan Clark's right to bring an AR-15 into the council meeting that night — direct quote: 'I do not believe that Council[man] Clark needs to apologize for exercising his right as a law enforcement officer to carry his weapon to a public meeting.' The 2020 resolution was explicitly framed as opposition to the then-Democratic-trifecta VA General Assembly's pending gun-control package (red-flag/AWB/UBC/mag-limits) — hard adverse to those restrictions. HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2 evidence: 2022 platform pledge 'a safe city with lower taxes' (campaign signature line per WAVY/Ballotpedia); voted YES on Portsmouth's FY25 budget that used Rivers Casino revenue to MAINTAIN a real-estate tax-rate DECREASE from $1.30 to $1.23 — same explicit-tax-cut framing the SKILL credited Mayor Glover for in the 1530 dossier (q2 TRUE precedent for Portsmouth councilmembers who voted YES on that budget). HARD TRUE public_justice q0 evidence (multi-vector affirmative pro-police advocacy, clears the high bar): (1) on 7/14/2020 he personally emailed Police Chief Angela Greene specifically demanding that the law be enforced and charges filed against the Confederate-monument vandals — NAACP attempted to have him criminally charged for the email, but a magistrate declined to issue process; (2) 2022 platform: 'The city is short 80 to 100 police officers… we need to maintain a full complement of 260 sworn officers' and restore the Community Impact Officer program; (3) publicly defended Councilman Nathan Clark's right to carry an AR-15 in chambers as a law-enforcement officer; (4) 2016: called BLM tunnel-blockers 'thugs' (later apologized — captures rhetorical posture against criminal-conduct framing). Multi-vector and quote-bearing — clears the affirmative-advocacy bar. HARD FALSE public_justice q3 evidence: voted YES on Portsmouth's gun-buyback program while admitting on the record 'studies show gun buyback programs aren't effective at obtaining guns from those who will use them to harm others' but adding 'it can't hurt' — i.e. acquiesced to the public-health framing of gun violence he himself didn't believe was efficacious. Affirmative participation in soft public-health framing → adverse on q3. HARD TRUE refuse_state_overreach q0 evidence: the 1/14/2020 2A resolution was an explicit assertion of local control against an unconstitutional state mandate the Democratic-trifecta VA General Assembly was preparing — Moody's direct quote: 'I can appreciate liberals' knee jerk reactions but if they have a problem with the law they should use the system they now control to change it.' Local-control posture against state overreach. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity of life (no abortion-stance statement; Portsmouth council does not vote on this), biblical_marriage q0/q2 (no marriage-definition or biological-sex statement), q4 (no Portsmouth council Pride proclamation vote has ever occurred — the 2022 Pride Festival was a private nonprofit event; absence of a vote is not scoreable per the 1530 SKILL discipline so q4 left null rather than provisionally TRUE), family_child_sovereignty (any cell — Portsmouth schools are governed by an elected School Board; Moody has no direct curriculum-vote record), christian_liberty q0/q2/q3/q4 (his 2016 FOIA-litigation win against his own council (Moody v. Portsmouth, Judge Hammond ruled the council violated FOIA when fining him $1,500 for disclosing closed-session monument discussions — quote: 'good news for anybody who supports open government and transparency') is a clean transparency/free-speech victory but the rubric's christian_liberty q4 cell is specifically about state-funded promotion of non-Christian religious displays, which the FOIA win does not address; no cell maps cleanly, so the FOIA narrative is captured in profile context and left unscored), election_integrity q0/q1/q4, public_justice q1/q2/q4 (no cash-bail / restitution / Romans-13 framing — his 'use the system' line is procedural-democracy framing, not Romans-13 magisterial-sword framing), refuse_state_overreach q1/q2/q3/q4 (no COVID-mandate-resistance / ESG / subsidiarity statement located beyond q0), border_immigration q1/q2 (no Moody statement on Portsmouth's May 2025 DHS sanctuary listing or Sheriff Moore's WSO/287(g) cancellation; his 2019 'Squad' tweet was racial-framing not immigration-policy and is not scoreable). Counter-archetype context (NOT scored, but on audit trail): 2016 BLM 'thugs' remark (apologized), 2019 'really tired of hearing the term people of color' / 'Squad' tweet, Dorris v. Glover/Moody pending wrongful-termination suit alleging tax-assessment interference at Elizabeth Manor Country Club — negative-evidence flags for any 'principled-statesman' framing but do not contradict his R-leaning Independent archetype. Fix party Unknown→I; set candidacy_status=general_candidate (running 8th term, May 27 2026 reversal). 5-TRUE/1-FALSE on cited cells (~83% on a 6-cell denominator) honestly reflects a cultural-conservative populist with a verbalized 2A/police/local-control record plus one go-along-to-get-along gun-buyback misstep — coherent, not a sparse-data 100/0.

2.note: 2022 platform pledge of 'a safe city with lower taxes'; voted YES on Portsmouth's FY25 budget which used Rivers Casino revenue to MAINTAIN a real-estate tax-rate decrease from $1.30 to $1.23 (same precedent as the Glover 1530 q2 TRUE — Portsmouth councilmembers who voted YES on the explicit tax-cut framing of casino revenue clear the local-rubric rate-restraint cell).

0.note: Multi-vector affirmative pro-police advocacy, clears the high bar: (1) 7/14/2020 personally emailed Police Chief Angela Greene demanding the law be enforced and charges filed against Confederate-monument vandals (NAACP attempted to charge him for it; magistrate declined to issue process); (2) 2022 platform 'we need to maintain a full complement of 260 sworn officers' + restore Community Impact Officers; (3) publicly defended Councilman Nathan Clark's right to carry an AR-15 in chambers as a law-enforcement officer. Quote- and action-bearing, beyond a budget vote.

3.note: Voted YES on Portsmouth's gun-buyback program while admitting on the record 'studies show gun buyback programs aren't effective at obtaining guns from those who will use them to harm others' but adding 'it can't hurt.' Affirmative participation in a public-health-flavored intervention he himself acknowledged was ineffective — acquiescence to public-health framing of violence the rubric scores against.

0.note: Voted YES on Portsmouth's 1/14/2020 4-3 'Second Amendment Constitutional City' resolution explicitly framed as local pushback against the Democratic-trifecta VA General Assembly's pending gun-control package. Moody quote: 'I can appreciate liberals' knee jerk reactions but if they have a problem with the law they should use the system they now control to change it.' Direct local-control assertion against an unconstitutional state mandate.

Voted YES on the 1/14/2020 4-3 'Second Amendment Constitutional City' resolution declaring Portsmouth a 2A sanctuary; publicly defended Councilman Nathan Clark's right to bring an AR-15 into chambers as a law-enforcement officer: 'I do not believe that Council[man] Clark needs to apologize for exercising his right as a law enforcement officer to carry his weapon to a public meeting.' Affirmative pro-constitutional/permitless-carry posture.

1.note: The 1/14/2020 2A resolution Moody voted YES on was explicitly framed in opposition to the VA General Assembly's then-pending Democratic gun-control package (red-flag/ERPO, assault-weapons ban, magazine limits, universal background checks) and pledged municipal non-cooperation — categorical opposition to red-flag/AWB/registries.

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND from any primary or reputable secondary source. profile.education: B.S. Chemical Engineering, University of Virginia. Project Management Professional (PMP) certification, April 2009.
profile.background: Portsmouth native. Married to Maceo Thomas (~15 years as of 2024); two sons. Career: Department of Energy (nuclear technology) → ABS Consulting (risk consultant, project manager) → TowneBank (VP, senior Operational Risk Analyst) → currently Cybersecurity Project Manager / GRC professional. Prior elected/appointed service: Portsmouth City School Board (elected 2020 with 13.4% in a 9-way race, served 2021-2024); Portsmouth City Planning Commission. Civic: Portsmouth Alumnae Chapter of Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Inc.; board of The M.A.N. Foundation (resume / job-training nonprofit for formerly incarcerated). Portsmouth City Council, at-large (officially nonpartisan, ran as Independent), elected Nov 5, 2024 as the top vote-getter at 15.91% in the at-large field; sworn 1/1/2025; term 1/1/2025-12/31/2028 — NOT on the Nov 2026 ballot. No formal R/D endorsement located; D-aligned by civic-network (Delta Sigma Theta, public-school advocacy, prisoner-reentry board work) but not by record.

yolanda-thomas.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Portsmouth City Council, at-large (officially nonpartisan, ran as Independent, D-aligned by civic network not by record). Term 1/1/2025-12/31/2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. NOT on council for the 1/14/2020 4-3 'Second Amendment Constitutional City' vote (she was on the Portsmouth School Board then; no Council 2A roll-call to score). Her campaign signals are all process-and-investment language — 'collaboration,' 'transformation,' 'community precincts,' 'first responder retention,' 'public education investment' — with zero hard signals on any of the 30 RESOLUTE Local cells in her ~18 months on council. Her 'five-point plan' pairs police 'with community precincts and social services outreach in high-crime, high-need areas' — soft public-health-adjacent framing, but the quote in her OWN voice never reaches the explicit 'public health crisis' phrasing the rubric scores against, and pairing police WITH social-services (rather than substituting for police) is on the line per Doyle/Bryant precedent — left null. Per the SKILL's sparse-data discipline + Doyle/Bryant precedent (single-vector progressive rhetoric without an independent endorsement vector or quoted culture-war stance does not clear FALSE or TRUE on a 1-cell denominator and would mislead), this record is left roster_verified with all 30 LOCAL-rubric cells null pending an accumulating record. NO QUOTE-BEARING POSITIONS LOCATED on sanctity of life (no abortion-stance statement), biblical_marriage (any cell — no statement on natural marriage, biological-sex/trans-youth policy, Pride proclamations;
